zh.Ashill, Norfolkshire, England, United Ki: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可阿希爾
zh.Cheadle, Staffordshire, England: 英國英格蘭斯塔福爾黑阿德爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret le Bigod於1182年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可特福德時，她的父親Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk38歲，母親Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk21歲。她於大約1202年與William de Hastings在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1237年3月28日享年55歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可特福德。

zh-hans.Ashill, Norfolkshire, England, United Ki: 英国英格兰諾福爾可阿希爾
zh-hans.Cheadle, Staffordshire, England: 英国英格兰斯塔福爾黑阿德爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret le Bigod于1182年出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可特福德时，她的父亲Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk38岁，母亲Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk21岁。她于大约1202年与William de Hastings在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1237年3月28日享年55岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可特福德。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Bigod
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jaroslaw, Jozef, Krystyna, Slawomir.

Polish and Jewish (from Poland): from bigos, formerlydenoting anything that was chopped or slashed, now a particular dishcomposed of hash and sauerkraut; also ‘confusion’; hence perhaps anickname for someone who caused confusion.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret le Bigod was born in 1182, in Thetford, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk, was 38 and her mother, Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk, was 21. She married William de Hastings about 1202, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 28 March 1237, at the age of 55, and was buried in Thetford, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret le Bigod est née en 1182, à Thetford, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé William de Hastings environ 1202, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 28 mars 1237, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterrée à Thetford, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret le Bigod 1182 in Thetford, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk 38 und ihre Mutter Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk 21. Sie hat um 1202 in England, United Kingdom William de Hastings geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 28. März 1237 im Alter von 55 Jahren und wurde in Thetford, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret le Bigod è nata il nel 1182 nel Thetford, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk, aveva 38 anni e sua madre, Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato William de Hastings circa 1202, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 28 marzo 1237, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolta a Thetford, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret le Bigodが1182年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクスエトフォードで生まれた時，父Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolkは38歳，母Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolkは21歳であった。約1202年にイギリスイギリスでWilliam de Hastingsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1237年3月28日に55歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクスエトフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret le Bigod(이)가 1182년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 테트퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 대략 1202년 영국 잉글랜드에서 William de Hastings(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1237년 3월 28일 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 테트퍼드에 묻혔다.

mn.Cheadle, Staffordshire, England: Cheadle, Stæffordscīr, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret le Bigod nasceu em 1182, em Thetford, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com William de Hastings aproximadamente 1202, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 28 de março de 1237, com 55 anos, e foi sepultada em Thetford, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret le Bigod родилась в 1182 г. в местности тетфорд, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William de Hastings около 1202 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 28 марта 1237 г. в возрасте 55 лет и была похоронена в местности тетфорд, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret le Bigod nació en 1182, en Thetford, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk, tenía 21 años. Se casó con William de Hastings aproximadamente 1202, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 28 de marzo de 1237, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultada en Thetford, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
